title: High Ercall area OTU Memorial
memorial_type: Various memorials associated with 60 OTU training losses

external_narratives.summary: National Archives records confirm No. 60 Operational Training Unit was formed at High Ercall in May 1943, training night intruder crews on de Havilland Mosquitoes. The unit suffered multiple training losses across the UK, from Shropshire to Northern Ireland and Wales.
key_details: 60 OTU formed at High Ercall May 1943
Trained night intruder crews on Mosquitoes

external_narratives.summary: RAF High Ercall served as a fighter station and later an OTU base during WW2. No. 60 OTU operated Mosquitoes from the airfield from 1943, with numerous training accidents recorded across mountainous terrain in Wales, Northern Ireland, and the Pennines.
key_details: Airfield served as fighter station before OTU role
Training accidents occurred across wide geographical area

external_narratives.summary: Aircrew Remembered documents several 60 OTU losses including Mosquito HJ650 flown by F/O Liblikmaa. The Estonian-born pilot was one of many international aircrew who trained at High Ercall, reflecting the multinational nature of Bomber Command's training pipeline.
key_details: International aircrew trained at High Ercall including Estonian pilots
